description: Canonical zones/regions for the Nannymud-inspired game world. Each zone has a category that determines PvP rules and gameplay purpose. MVP ships with 1 hub + 1 PvE zone + 1 PvP arena + 1 Outlands zone.

categoryRules.hub: Persistent town. Safe zone. PvP disabled. PK-flagged players are auto-attacked by guards on sight. Bank, merchants, NPCs, quest givers live here.
categoryRules.pve-instance: Instanced per party. PvP disabled. For quests and coop adventure.
categoryRules.pvp-arena: Consensual PvP. Matchmade instance. No PK flag consequence. Cosmetic rewards + meta XP.
categoryRules.outlands: Open-PvP instanced zone. You can be attacked by any other player. Killing someone here triggers the PK flag on the killer. Loot-on-death active.

zones.loreSummary: The largest city in the mainland. Ruled by the King and Queen of Ghan-Toth. Neutral ground — all guilds welcome. Houses the Central Bank of Ghan-Toth and the Grand Bazaar.

neighborhoods.note: Central Bank. Item storage + gold vault. Taxed withdrawal after death events.
Entry portals to each of the 6 MVP guilds.
The Maple Tree Pub. Where players form parties and post contracts.

zones.loreSummary: Rolling grasslands stretching from Ghan-Toth to the horizon. Bandit camps, roaming pegasi, and old ruins dot the plain. The newbie adventuring ground.
A colosseum beneath Ghan-Toth, built by Mats himself. Champions clash for honor, glory, and matchmaking rank.

modes.note: Core MOBA mode. Capture three shrines, hold them to drain the enemy nexus.

zones.loreSummary: The lawless borderland. Dragons prowl the peaks above; bandits and killers prowl the valleys below. The richest ore veins in the world sit here — but so do the best reasons to die.
zones.pvpRules: Open PvP. PK flag applied to killer. Loot-on-death: winner picks one item from corpse + all carried gold.

zones.loreSummary: Primeval forest where druids gather. Home to Laurana's Pool, the Kerish Roses, and the Ash Tree Giant's Lair.
Monks of the Holy Order train here beneath swaying green shafts. The Cathbad Abbey stands at its heart.
Fog-shrouded highlands. Vampires stalk the night. Kefka's and Angmar's castles loom through the mist.
Undead marsh. The path to the Drowned Cult's coastal shrines. (Drowned Cult = IP-sanitized Cthulhu guild domain.)
Cold northern sea. Viking longships raid its coasts. Isles of Hope and Despair, Casada, Xenios rise from its waves.
The second continent, reachable only by portal from the Mages Guild hall. Endgame zone with late-game dungeons.
Magical island populated by the Simyarin. Mages pilgrimage here to deepen their craft.
Small farming village west of Ghan-Toth. A minor hub with a single merchant and an inn. Lower-population alternative to Ghan-Toth for roleplay-oriented players.
Coastal fishing village. Gateway to the Sea of Claws voyages. Shipwrights sell rowboats here.
Frozen northern reaches mapped on the Earendil '98 world map. Ice giants, mammoths, and frost cultists roam this endless white. No safe roads — only cairns.
A rugged range marked on the old hand-drawn map. Miners, mountain dwarves, and a hidden monastery clinging to the cliffs.
Marshy coastal region labeled on the Earendil map. Fishing hamlets, marsh-hags, and the ruins of a sunken lighthouse.
Desert trade city from the old world map. A caravanserai hub where merchants from the southern sands meet those from the mainland. Fewer guilds, exotic wares.
Mountainous region north of Ghan-Toth on the Earendil map. Hermit outposts and snowbound ruins. Entry point to the tundra above.
A cursed region on the old map. The name itself means death. Necropolis, lich-lords, and restless barrows. Endgame zone.
Farming province south of Ghan-Toth on the Earendil map. Orchards, millers, and bandit raids on the backroads. Good for low-level quests.
One of the twin isles in the hand-drawn island map. Bright cliffs, ruined temple, a single NPC scholar cataloguing a lost civilization.
Twin isle to Hope. Open-PvP. Shipwrecks, pirates, and a black tower. Legend says no one who lands alone returns.

earendil-1998-world.note: The canonical mainland map used by the Nannymud community. Regions inferred from visible labels.

hand-drawn-islands.note: Depicts the Sea of Claws archipelago.

pixel-colored-map.note: More modern rendering of the same world.
